title: Customer Success Manager (Junior)
required_skills: Ability to manage multiple customers simultaneously
Comfortable with ambiguity and early-stage environments
bonus_skills: B2B SaaS customer success or account management experience
Familiarity with construction tech or enterprise software
matched_skills: Strong communication skills (C-level reporting, trade fair & meeting presentation, sales demo & user training)
Customer-facing experience (Futu Holdings: sales demo & user training; Leyard: 20+ channel partners training)
Organized and detail-oriented (Agile project management, milestone tracking at Hapag-Lloyd)
Voice-of-customer research (maps to gathering user feedback for product team)
KPI dashboards and data analysis (maps to monitoring usage patterns)
missing_skills: Comfortable with ambiguity and early-stage startup environments (not directly evidenced)
B2B SaaS customer success or CSM tooling experience
Familiarity with construction tech or enterprise software
Support ticket / live chat operations experience
core_responsibilities: Onboard new customers and guide users through first steps with the product
Monitor support requests across tickets and live chat, ensuring timely responses
Create onboarding materials and maintain knowledge base for new feature releases
Monitor usage patterns and derive insights to drive adoption and user value
Gather customer feedback and synthesize insights for the product team
recommended_emphasis: Highlight customer-facing roles at Futu Holdings and Leyard: user training, demo delivery, and partner enablement directly map to onboarding responsibilities
Emphasize voice-of-customer research and translating user insights to product teams (Hapag-Lloyd KPI analysis, cross-functional interface at HSBC)
Showcase ability to create structured materials: sales collateral, demo scripts, training content at Leyard for 20+ partners
Stress comfort with fast-moving, ambiguous environments — MBA + multi-industry background signals adaptability
Mention HubSpot/Salesforce and Jira/Confluence as tools relevant to CS operations and knowledge base management
